name: About
description: Hello Vietnam about page — intro, origin-story timeline, crew, values, impact, contact band.

fields.heading: We're not a tour company. We're your loud Vietnamese cousins.
fields.body_p1: Hello Vietnam started over a beer in 2016, when Linh realized most tourists were seeing her country through a tinted bus window. Ten years later, we've hand-built 18 trips across Vietnam — all led by people who grew up here.
fields.body_p2: We skip the tourist traps. We pay our guides fairly. We eat lunch on the same tiny plastic stools as our guests.

team_photo.alt: Warm portrait of Linh, founder of Hello Vietnam

fields.team_photo_caption: team photo · lunch on plastic stools

entries.body: Linh quits her bank job. Starts leading food tours for backpacker friends.
Tuan joins after driving Linh through Ha Giang on a whim. The loop is born.
Pandemic hits. We pivot to online cooking classes and stay connected.
We open a Hanoi office. Still just a room above a bánh mì shop.
2,400+ travelers later, we're still on plastic stools.

items.title: No tourist traps
items.body: If it's in every guidebook, we're not taking you there. Period.

items.title: Supporting locals
items.body: Every vendor we work with is a neighbor. Every guide earns a real wage.

items.title: Always sustainable
items.body: Small groups, reusable everything, and trips that leave places better.

fields.heading: 5% of every booking funds a village school.
fields.body: Since 2019, we've helped build two classrooms and funded 47 scholarships in Ba Be Province. Our partner, the Ma Pi Leng Learning Initiative, is run by former guide Minh.

image.alt: Vietnamese kids waving hello in a mountain village

fields.heading: Got a question?
fields.body: We answer every WhatsApp within a few hours. Promise.
